# Inspect AI

[Inspect AI](https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/) is an open-source LLM evaluation framework developed by the UK AI Safety Institute. It provides tasks, datasets, scorers, and agent evaluation workflows for structured benchmark-style evaluation. MLflow's Inspect AI integration allows you to use Inspect AI scorers as MLflow scorers, logging results, comparing runs, and viewing assessments alongside traces in MLflow.

## Prerequisites

Inspect AI scorers require the `inspect-ai` package:

```bash
pip install inspect-ai
```

## Quick Start

You can call an Inspect AI scorer directly using <APILink fn="mlflow.genai.scorers.inspect_ai.get_scorer">get_scorer</APILink>:

```python
from mlflow.genai.scorers.inspect_ai import get_scorer

scorer = get_scorer("model_graded_fact", model="openai/gpt-4o-mini")
feedback = scorer(
    inputs="What is the capital of France?",
    outputs="Paris is the capital of France.",
    expectations={"target": "Paris"},
)

print(feedback.value)    # "yes" or "no"
print(feedback.rationale)
print(feedback.metadata["terminal_state"])  # "scored", "skip", "not_run", or "error"
```

Or use scorers inside <APILink fn="mlflow.genai.evaluate">mlflow.genai.evaluate</APILink>:

```python
import mlflow
from mlflow.genai.scorers.inspect_ai import get_scorer

eval_dataset = [
    {
        "inputs": {"question": "What is the capital of France?"},
        "outputs": "Paris is the capital of France.",
        "expectations": {"target": "Paris"},
    },
    {
        "inputs": {"question": "What is 2 + 2?"},
        "outputs": "4",
        "expectations": {"target": "4"},
    },
]

results = mlflow.genai.evaluate(
    data=eval_dataset,
    scorers=[
        get_scorer("model_graded_fact", model="openai/gpt-4o-mini"),
    ],
)
```

## Using `get_scorer`

<APILink fn="mlflow.genai.scorers.inspect_ai.get_scorer">get_scorer</APILink> accepts any Inspect AI scorer or task name registered in the installed package:

```python
from mlflow.genai.scorers.inspect_ai import get_scorer

# Named Inspect AI scorer
scorer = get_scorer(
    metric_name="model_graded_fact",   # Inspect AI scorer/task name
    model="openai/gpt-4o-mini",        # Model URI for LLM-judge scorers
    model_kwargs={"temperature": 0.0}, # Optional model parameters
)
```

Additional keyword arguments are forwarded directly to the Inspect AI scorer. Refer to the [Inspect AI scorer documentation](https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/scorers.html) for scorer-specific parameters.

## Terminal-State Semantics

MLflow preserves Inspect AI's evaluation states explicitly. Every `Feedback` object returned by an Inspect AI scorer includes a `terminal_state` key in its `metadata` field:

| `terminal_state` | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `"scored"` | Scorer ran successfully and produced a result |
| `"skip"` | Case was explicitly skipped by the scorer |
| `"not_run"` | Scorer returned no result (case was not evaluated) |
| `"error"` | Scorer reported an error (distinct from a model failure) |

This lets you distinguish between a model producing a wrong answer and an evaluator that failed independently:

```python
feedback = scorer(inputs="...", outputs="...")

match feedback.metadata["terminal_state"]:
    case "scored":
        print(f"Score: {feedback.value}")
    case "skip":
        print("Case was skipped")
    case "not_run":
        print("Scorer did not evaluate this case")
    case "error":
        print(f"Scorer error: {feedback.rationale}")
```

Scorer runtime exceptions (crashes, timeouts) are recorded as `Feedback.error` rather than disappearing silently from the evaluation aggregate.

## Configuration

### Model URIs

The `model` parameter accepts any MLflow model URI format:

```python
# OpenAI
scorer = get_scorer("model_graded_fact", model="openai/gpt-4o")

# Databricks-hosted endpoint
scorer = get_scorer("model_graded_fact", model="databricks/my-llm-endpoint")

# Default model from environment
scorer = get_scorer("model_graded_fact")
```

### Multi-Turn (Session) Evaluation

Inspect AI scorers that operate on conversational sessions accept a `session` parameter — a list of MLflow `Trace` objects representing the conversation turns:

```python
scorer = get_scorer("conversational_task", model="openai/gpt-4o-mini")

feedback = scorer(
    session=[trace_turn_1, trace_turn_2, trace_turn_3],
    expectations={"goal": "book a flight to Paris"},
)
```
